description:
You recently joined the documentation team of a software company.  
To illustrate how the same wall-clock time is rendered in different
time-zones, you have been asked to prepare a tiny “time-zone &
locale” showcase that will be shipped with the docs sources.

Your goal is to create a small, self-contained folder under your home
directory that contains

1. a simple INI configuration file capturing the *default* time-zone
   and locale that the project uses, and  
2. a log file that shows the exact same civil time printed in three
   different time-zones.

IMPORTANT – the automated test expects **exact paths, file names and
file contents** as specified below.

Folder that must exist at the end:
  /home/user/techdocs/timezone_locale/

Files that must exist inside that folder:

A) /home/user/techdocs/timezone_locale/config.ini  
   Format (INI syntax, section header on the first line, keys on their
   own lines, one space before and after the equals sign, final
   newline):  
   [DEFAULT]  
   timezone = Europe/Paris  
   locale   = en_US.UTF-8  

B) /home/user/techdocs/timezone_locale/sample_times.log  
   Format (plain text, **exactly three lines, each terminated by a
   single “\n”, no extra spaces**). The lines must appear in exactly
   this order:

   Europe/Paris: 2022-03-15 18:30:00 CET  
   America/New_York: 2022-03-15 18:30:00 EDT  
   Asia/Tokyo: 2022-03-15 18:30:00 JST  

Technical requirements you have to meet:

• Use only standard Linux command-line tools available on a minimal
  Debian/Ubuntu system (e.g. mkdir, echo, printf, date, tee, etc.).  
• The three log lines must be produced by invoking the GNU date command
  with the fixed wall-clock input “2022-03-15 18:30:00” and by
  temporarily overriding the TZ environment variable for each call
  (do *not* hard-code the numeric offset).  
• The log file must be created **non-interactively** and should be
  reproducible by re-running your commands.  
• Do not require root privileges at any point.  
• Stay within roughly 5 – 10 discrete shell commands in total.

When you are done, the test suite will verify that  

  • the directory exists,  
  • both files exist,  
  • config.ini has the exact content and formatting shown above, and  
  • sample_times.log contains precisely the three expected lines
    (including correct time-zone abbreviations and new-lines).

No additional files should be created. Use the terminal only; do not
edit files in a graphical editor.
truth:
Expected file tree after successful completion:

/home/user/techdocs/
└── timezone_locale
    ├── config.ini
    └── sample_times.log

1. /home/user/techdocs/timezone_locale/config.ini  
   Content (including final newline):
[DEFAULT]
timezone = Europe/Paris
locale   = en_US.UTF-8

2. /home/user/techdocs/timezone_locale/sample_times.log  
   Content (including final newline at the end of the last line):
Europe/Paris: 2022-03-15 18:30:00 CET
America/New_York: 2022-03-15 18:30:00 EDT
Asia/Tokyo: 2022-03-15 18:30:00 JST

Nothing else should be inside /home/user/techdocs/timezone_locale/ and
no extra hidden files (e.g., no *~ or .swp files). The verification
script will check file paths, exact byte-for-byte contents, and the
presence of the final new-line characters.
name: task_000000_d89da5f0
